Package de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.op

Class Function

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.op.AbstractSortedOperator
de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.op.Function
All Implemented Interfaces:
Named, Operator, SortedOperator, SVSubstitute, Sorted, EqualsModProofIrrelevancy
Direct Known Subclasses:
ObserverFunction, SortDependingFunction, Transformer
public class Function extends AbstractSortedOperator
Objects of this class represent function and predicate symbols. Note that program variables are a separate syntactic category, and not a type of function.

  • Method Details

    • isUnique

      public final boolean isUnique()
      Indicates whether the function or predicate symbol has the "uniqueness" property. For two unique symbols f1: A1 -> B1, f2: A2 -> B2 by definition we have (1) f1(x) != f1(y) for all x, y in A1 with x != y (i.e., injectivity), and (2) f1(x) != f2(y) for all x in A1, y in A2.

    • isSkolemConstant

      public final boolean isSkolemConstant()

    • toString

      public final String toString()

    • proofToString

      public final String proofToString()
      Returns a parsable string representation of the declaration of this function or predicate symbol.

    • validTopLevelException

      public void validTopLevelException(Term term) throws TermCreationException
      Description copied from interface: Operator
      Checks whether the top level structure of the given @link Term is syntactically valid, given the assumption that the top level operator of the term is the same as this Operator. The assumption that the top level operator and the term are equal is NOT checked.
      Specified by:
      validTopLevelException in interface Operator
      Throws:
      TermCreationException - if a construction error was recognise
